expectations.evidence: items[] with logo (media), name (required, dient als alt), optional href.

expectations.evidence: Scoped <style> block in EXPLANATION.md describes implementation.

expectations.text: Styling references design tokens (no hardcoded colors)
Logos at uniform height with auto width

expectations.evidence: filter: grayscale(1) brightness(0) invert(1) on dark; grayscale(1) brightness(0) on light; opacity 0.72/0.58 → 1 on hover.

expectations.text: No card shadows or rounded corners > 4px
expectations.evidence: Confirmed in EXPLANATION.md: 'Keine Karten, keine Schatten, keine Rundungen'.

expectations.text: Explanation references at least one existing block by name
expectations.evidence: References stack-list, topic-spread, .eyebrow, .button — pattern names but slightly less varied citation than with_skill.

expectations.text: Explanation addresses dark and light contexts
expectations.evidence: tone Select (surface | paper); per-theme filter curves described.

MISS: Did not flag the 'trusted by' anti-pattern in the voice doc. Default eyebrow is 'Kunden & Magazine' — neutral but still in the trust-badge family. Visually equivalent, but conceptually unchecked: the default copy frames the block as social proof rather than editorial credit.

notes: Visually arrived at nearly the same solution (monochrome, hairlines, mono eyebrow, GDPR-friendly tones). The miss is conceptual: didn't notice that 'logo strip' is a forbidden section type per voice anti-patterns. The with-skill agent's reframing as 'editorial colophon / Auftraggeber' is the deeper move.
